message:
fix(e2e,parser,vdbe,vfs): switch default VFS to linux-uring-fs, parameterize SSI account count, harden lexer exponent validation, and improve VDBE/uring error handling

Default VFS feature (fsqlite-e2e/Cargo.toml):
- Switch default feature from linux-asupersync-uring to linux-uring-fs
  for broader compatibility. The asupersync backend requires the separate
  asupersync runtime which isn't always available in CI/local builds.

SSI test parameterization (bd_3plop_5_ssi_serialization_correctness.rs):
- Add configurable ACCOUNT_COUNT with debug/release variants (1024/4096)
  and env override (FSQLITE_SSI_ACCOUNT_COUNT) to prevent page-hotspot
  collapse in debug builds with fewer writers
- Thread account_count through initialize_db, run_worker,
  execute_single_txn, and random_account
- Add env_i64 helper for parsing environment overrides

Lexer exponent validation (fsqlite-parser/lexer.rs):
- Add is_valid_exponent helper that checks the full exponent syntax
  (e/E followed by optional +/- followed by at least one digit) before
  consuming. Previously, a bare 'e' at end of input or 'e' followed by
  non-digit would be consumed as part of the number literal, producing
  an invalid token.

VDBE engine and uring VFS improvements for error handling robustness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13,7 +13,7 @@ description = "End-to-end differential testing and benchmark harness for Franken
 workspace = true
 
 [features]
-default = ["linux-asupersync-uring"]
+default = ["linux-uring-fs"]
 linux-uring-fs = [
     "fsqlite/linux-uring-fs",
     "fsqlite-core/linux-uring-fs",
